item.title: O5-6 is sitting on a Unversal Instant "I Win" Button and doesn't even realize it.
item.content:encoded:
I read this yesterday and thought it was a pretty good SCP, so I upvoted it.

But today I've been hit with some Fridge Logic. If this thing changes into whatever people believe it to be, then O5-6 could just convince people that it's something that could destroy 682. or something that can repair the chains of 2317. Or a map to the the keys that lock Able in his bedroom. Or a more permanent and humane alternative to Procedure 110 Montauk. Or even a document explaining where Anomalies come from. Literally any obstacle the Foundation faces could be overcome by convincing people that this thing is a means of surmounting it. So I'm changing my vote to a downvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, can't be bothered to write more about a chair though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It’s $399 that’s a great price!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I down at first.Because I did not see that red words.
OH,is just a chair!BUT the point is high!oh.wait.up!

item.title: Audio Adaptation
item.content:encoded:
I enjoy the "thing that isn't a thing" kind of a story.

Here's the reading.
https://youtu.be/02H_Mqj2jtc

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

The story being told here is largely self contained, but that is not to say that some crosslinks can't be worked in. I see that at one point there was a link to 055 added, but it was removed, so we'll avoid that suggestion. The mention of the Serpent's Hand can be turned into an inline link to their hub. Additionally, the final mention of O5-6 can be turned into a link to the often overlooked O5 Dossier that exists now, and offers a glimpse of numerous canons as to who each O5 is. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

A secret meeting saw the O5’s and the Serpent’s Hand discussing what SCP-2950 was. They showed us their books and their stories that gave us what we needed to know. A lot of us were skeptical. More of us were desperate.

To this:

A secret meeting saw the O5’s and the Serpent’s Hand discussing what SCP-2950 was. They showed us their books and their stories that gave us what we needed to know. A lot of us were skeptical. More of us were desperate.

Changing this:

Good luck, sir or madam. The world needs you on this one.

And, by the way, congratulations on the promotion.

O5-6

To this:

Good luck, sir or madam. The world needs you on this one.

And, by the way, congratulations on the promotion.

O5-6

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It isnt a chair and i believe its a shape shifter. It could totally be a portal like from the Constantine movie. I can't believe its a just a chair. If it really is an O5 chair then its still not a chair and has secrets hidden in it.

item.title: Chair.
item.content:encoded:
This object is pretty boring. It's just a chair that makes people sit on it. That's on the Big List of Cliches. Which I will refer to when I downvote this piece, because thing that make you do thing is a ridiculously overused concept…-1.

+  
- C H A I R !

This is actually pretty cool. The concept of "look at this boring chair that…PSYCHE! IT'S DOOM INCARNATE MWAHAHAHAHAHAHHAHA!" was…ridiculous. But I'm a fan of ridiculous, so this got a +1 from me.

BTW. Collapsible chair…as someone pointed out…is now pun of the year.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ha! Collapsible chair!
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
can't say im a fan of this gimmick. you just went like

"hey look at this mundane chair that just happens to be comfy! JUST KIDDING! its actually some Keter infohazard thing that only O5-6 can know about! also the Serpent's hand is involved!"

really a stupid gimmick in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As it turns out, this was the original O5-6's favorite chair in the world, but they didn't want anyone else to use it ever (why? It was their favorite chair and they deserved it the most!). So to make sure no one else would use it, before they retired/died, they came up with SCP-2950, and concoted a story where no one would use it. Just out of spite. This is canon and no one can tell me otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In retrospect, this hinges too much on the cover-up article aspect to stand on its own. Let's put it at neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't be the only one thinking of Ghostbusters here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a concept that has been done several times before, and it doesn't do anything new with it.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a hard idea to do well but you nailed it. This is excellent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Informally "Redact All Important Shit Always" but probably not to their face.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. You see more of them in SCP-076, SCP-2998, SCP-835, Cursed, Like We Were Ever Kindergarten Teachers to Start With, and SCUTTLE. I can't think of many stories where they're actually prominent, but I tried to include their most notable appearances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Foundation Records And Information Security Administration… I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nobody, not RAISA,

Who's RAISA?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

Like the concept, but think it's more on amusing side rather than horror or creepy.

Despite the message claim it's Keter, I've feeling that the thing is pretty much contained.

If it take form and attributes of what most people think of it, and hundreds of people in Foundation and Serpent's Hand believe that it's a chair with only two persons know what it actually is, then unless there're new batch of books that reach even more people, there should be no problem.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I mean I'm starting to get tired of Thing That Makes You Use It, but the fact that it's a foldable chair made it charming enough for me to ignore the cliché.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've never liked SCPs that are just long messages telling the reader directly what it is, but I would be willing to forgive that if the effect was suitably unique. However, I've this one or something like it several times before. I don't think this is anything new or particularly interesting. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given that the article is currently rated +182, it seems to be quite effective at the length it is now.

For the record, there is no magic wordcount for SCPs that guarantees success. Adding more material could potentially bloat an article down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of want to see some history about the chair. It would make it longer and more interesting, plus a way to add some sensible and tolerable humor.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This brings nothing to the table that other SCPs haven't already done. Indeed, I'm struggling to see any significant difference between this and 2006.

Reading (or in some cases, rereading) the highest rated articles this year, I'm noticing that "let's give a description of one paragraph, followed by a sixteen paragraph-long addendum/interview/letter to the editor." While this certainly has its place, I have become increasingly sick of reading this style, and I feel that this article is a perfect example of it.

-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I didn't really like it. Reminded me too much of other SCPs, like 055. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing a not-insignificant proportion of those people won't notice the tags either :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yay, finally an evil chair. I approve of this piece.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Funny you should mention that — there's another article on the site that's heavily implied to be Slenderman, or something very similar, and it uses almost that exact concept!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like I had seen SCPs like this before, more than once. Also, I am quite disappointed that the O5-note did not do anything for me, I don't like the tone and the concept of the note, so -1 for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You ever watch Ghostbusters? Near the end of the movie, the big bad guy allows the Ghostbusters the opportunity to select the form that it will take for destroying the world. Despite them trying not to think of a form, one of them inadvertently picks the Stay-Puff Marshmallow Man as the destroyer.

That's basically what will happen if everyone knew it was a polymorph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Dude, flaming's not allowed here, since it's pretty much harassment/unnecessary vehemence/something staff will step in and call out and/or implement bans for.

It's fine to say "Hey, this concept makes me think of how people perceive Slenderman". You're expressing your opinion without insulting the author or anyone else. This is okay.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a thought I have on this that I KNOW will get me flamed just for thinking of mentioning it, but… oh well, nothing ventured, nothing gained. *Ahem*

This is what I've always seen Slenderman as being. Think about it, an entity that takes on the properties of whatever the majority of people believe it to be. At its core, that's what I believe the creature is. That may not be a popular view, but it works for me, and for that reason I really, REALLY like this one. Creatures that are dependent on belief have always been my favorites.

But, enough of my ranting on a completely unrelated subject. +1, didn't think anyone could write an SCP about a comfy chair so well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, the twist reminds me of SCP-1055 a bit, but I still like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I thought "it's a comfortable metal chair, which is already impossible, so I like it anyway just on principle."

Then there was the document explaining that there is a rabbit-hole there, that goes far deeper than the mere perception of the SCP being an impossibly comfy metal chair.

Mind = blown. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recommend skipping to 1:36 on the video for best results.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree that it's pretty obvious the chair isn't the actual SCP, but I also think that just adds to the idea of "ITS JUST A CHAIR, NOTHING TO SEE HERE" so it works well IMO
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Saw it coming, but goddamn you pulled it off superbly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Your flying carpet will continue to fly, so long as you do not think of an elephant."

CRASH!!!!

If you tell everyone that a scip can be a Keter-level threat, but won't so long as they don't think about it being one, it'll destroy the Site within ten minutes, tops.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The cover-up seems very….cover-uppy. It just seems a bit too obvious that it's not an actual SCP. Not really sure how to fix that, though, unless you wanted to write a mid-sized decently drafted scip.

The letter was very nice though. I'll throw you an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's relatively easy for someone to leave a note saying "Hey, check out SCP-2950" for their replacement O5.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My thoughts as I read this:
1) So, this is the World's Most Comfortable Chair?
2) That's impossible! Folding metal chairs are not comfortable. Not even anomalous ones.
3) So the previous O5-6 left a note to his/her successor, inside the most mundane SCP imaginable?
What are the chances that the next O5-6 is ever going to see it?

I gave out an upvote for this, but to be honest I'm too generous in handing those out. I get why this is really a keter-level SCP and why disseminating the anomalously comfortable chair cover story, is an effective containment procedure. What I don't get is why it would be appropriate to hide containment procedures for a keter-level SCP inside a document that the one person designated to implement them, may or may not ever read.

Edit: "may not ever read again (as an O5)".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think Roget summed up my thoughts about this pretty well, but I'm gonna stay neutral on it. It's just not doing it for me. Maybe we've had too many like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having more people know about this simply gives cause and chances for errors to be made. Yes, more people know about it, so they can think that it's a chair. But there'll always be that little nagging doubt in your mind, because you know that it's really not a chair.

You may attempt to convince yourself that, "It's a chair. It's just a chair." But within you, you'll know that it's not a chair, and that doubt will eat away at you. What if you slip up, and accidentally think that it's not a chair for a moment? What if your thoughts betray you and cause an unspeakable horror to be unleashed on the world?

What if the world ends, and it's all your fault?

/me turns off spooky music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The original entity was one thing, so to speak. It was one solid mass. Now, people think that it's a non-sapient chair. The entity has therefore changed its structure to match a chair.

However, if people were to think that it's a disparate entity composed of multiple parts, the entity as a whole would split into those disparate parts, with each having similar properties to the original singular entity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A companion tale may or may not be coming out with this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a weakness for the Foundation keeping secrets from itself, especially when it's as well done as this one, but there's one logical hole that bugs me. The need for secrecy. If it's a polymorph that becomes what the majority of people think it is, wouldn't letting people know its a polymorph render it safe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't like the particular device being used here, and I'm a little disappointed by the stuff under the collapsible - you give the plot synopsis of a potentially interesting story, but the plot synopsis itself isn't very interesting (as well as somewhat unclear), and I kind of wish that story was a tale rather than as an addendum, because we miss out on actually watching this whole thing unfold.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You jackass. This is freaking brilliant! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, kinda neutral on this. The cover-up justification is indeed very similar to Zach's, and there's a bit of a logical hole in the whole piece. How is this entity being identified as the thing that is a chair? The designation "SCP-2950" seems tenuous linkage between the polymorphic entity and the idea of chairness.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2950 is a chair.

I am Ryan Gosling.

This statement is fact

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't get all this wild-ass discussion and heated conflict over stuff and "mindfucks". It's just a sweet, comforting chair. You act like it's some kind of hidden nuclear hard-to-destroy chair with end-of-the-world ramifications if it ever stops being a comforty chair.

Chillax, mengs.
UR-E88R CONFIRMED

Now I'm wondering what kind of hilarious fun there is to be had if people read through articles without going through the collapsibles/logs/hidden prizes, checked out their tags, and only then check out the hidden prize

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I fall just short of downvoting on this for similar reasons. It's simple, it's a nice combination, it's not poorly done. It's bland, but I just couldn't bring myself to hit -1 after hovering over it the whole time I was reading the article. I like the idea of a new O5 sitting down to read the inbox piled high with books and letters from predecessors giving the down-low and this tickled that just well enough.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I kind of wanted to downvote because this isn't the newest idea in the world, it sort of reminds me of a combination of star signals, 055, and zachary callahan all boiled down into their simplest form. But I found it charming enough in its simplicity, that I upvoted. Y'know? Sometimes I don't need one of the longest and most mindfucking articles on the site to tell me a story I've heard before. Sometimes I just want a comfy chair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suppose the reference to SCP-055 can be removed. Doing so now.

I used the Serpent's Hand specifically because they are an organization that is devoted to knowing things. The Library serves as a repository for knowledge and wonder, and they would be the individuals who would most likely understand/know about this sort of thing. I also don't care for the idea that the Foundation and certain GOI's are intrinsically hostile all of the time, so I felt like using a traditionally more hostile GOI to break that trend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You've pulled this trick before, where the skip is scary and dangerous because we're told directly that it's scary and dangerous. I didn't like it before, and I certainly don't like it now. There is no intrinsic interest to what you're writing about. It's just "well if you believe it's something horribly dangerous, then that's exactly what it is OMG".

The ill-advised cross reference to SCP-055 just underlines that by calling attention to an article that does this much better, mostly because 055's punch comes from the fact that no one can remember it, rather than the "oh it could be anything" angle. Also it makes no sense that O5-6 would even be able to make that reference in his letter.

And why the Serpent's Hand? Are they just a thing we invoke now when we need sources for strange and mysterious things, but we don't want to spend time actually dealing with background details?

I don't care for this one at all. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I Am A Chair.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who doesn't love nice and simple SCP's that are just chairs and nothing else?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice and simple. Kind of an old-school Thing What Compels You To Use It feel, but I can deal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All that jibbering under the collapsible distracts from the chair. I doubt anyone will even want to read it when faced with a chair of anomalous comfort
